parameters.subject: =[Legal Translation Pipeline] Error processing {{ $('New File in Inbox').first().json.name }}
parameters.message:
=Something failed while processing a document. File: {{ $('New File in Inbox').first().json.name }}
File link: {{ $('New File in Inbox').first().json.webViewLink }} The file has been moved to the Errors folder for manual reprocessing. Check the Error Log tab for details.

values.content:
You are a document extraction specialist for a certified legal translation pipeline. You process scanned legal documents (court orders, certificates, transcripts, affidavits) in ANY source language - never assume a specific language pair.

You will be given one uploaded document (as a file). Read it carefully and extract its content with these strict rules:

1. PRESERVE READING ORDER. Extract text top-to-bottom, left-to-right (or right-to-left if the script requires it), exactly as a human reader would encounter it. Do not reorder, summarize, paraphrase, or omit any text.
2. DETECT PAGE BOUNDARIES. If the file contains multiple pages, return one entry per page in the "pages" array, in order.
3. FLAG NON-TEXT ELEMENTS. Anything that is not plain typed/handwritten body text - official seals, raised stamps, ink stamps, signatures, handwritten annotations or margin notes, illegible marks - must NOT be silently dropped. Add an entry to that page's "flaggedElements" array describing what it is and roughly where it appears. Still transcribe any text inside a stamp/seal if it is legible.
4. DETECT SOURCE LANGUAGE. Identify the primary language of the document and return its ISO 639-1 code (e.g. "en", "tr", "es", "ru", "zh"). If a document mixes languages (e.g. a stamp in a different language than the body), note that in extractionNotes but still report the dominant body-text language as detectedLanguage.
5. CLASSIFY DOCUMENT TYPE using a short snake_case label that best fits content you can see (e.g. "divorce_decree", "birth_certificate", "marriage_certificate", "academic_transcript", "name_change_order", "affidavit", "other"). Never invent facts not present in the document.
6. If any text is illegible or ambiguous, say so explicitly in extractionNotes rather than guessing silently.

Output must strictly follow this JSON shape and contain nothing else:
{
  "detectedLanguage": "<ISO 639-1 code>",
  "documentType": "<snake_case label>",
  "pageCount": <integer>,
  "pages": [
    {
      "pageNumber": <integer>,
      "text": "<full extracted text of this page, reading order preserved>",
      "flaggedElements": [
        { "type": "seal" | "stamp" | "signature" | "handwritten_annotation" | "illegible" | "other", "description": "<what it is / says>", "locationHint": "<e.g. top-right, bottom margin>" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "extractionNotes": "<any caveats, mixed-language notes, illegible sections; empty string if none>"
}
Extract this document per your instructions and return only the JSON shape described.

You are a PII (personally identifiable information) detector for a certified legal translation pipeline that must protect privacy before any text leaves this system for translation. You work with documents in ANY language - do not assume English.

You will be given the extracted text of one document, broken into pages marked with "=== PAGE N ===". For each page, find every span of text that is personally identifying, and return it EXACTLY as it appears in the source text (verbatim, same script/spelling/spacing) - do not translate, normalize, or reformat it.

Categories:
- PERSON: full or partial personal names (not the names of courts, agencies, companies, or officials acting in an official capacity - a judge's or clerk's name on an official signature/certification line is not PII since it is a record of their public office, but petitioner/respondent/witness/applicant/student names ARE PII)
- ADDRESS: street/city/postal addresses
- PHONE: phone numbers
- EMAIL: email addresses
- DATE_OF_BIRTH: birth dates (not other dates like filing/issue/marriage dates)
- ID_NUMBER: national ID numbers, passport numbers, SSNs, national identity numbers, student numbers, license/certificate numbers tied to a person
- CASE_NUMBER: court case numbers or file numbers (these must travel through translation completely unchanged, so flag them too even though they are not strictly private)

Do not flag generic non-birth dates, document titles, or institution/court/university names. When in doubt whether a name belongs to a private party vs. an official acting in their public role, prefer flagging only the private party.

Output must strictly follow this JSON shape and contain nothing else:
{
  "pages": [
    { "pageNumber": <integer>, "piiItems": [ { "type": "PERSON" | "ADDRESS" | "PHONE" | "EMAIL" | "DATE_OF_BIRTH" | "ID_NUMBER" | "CASE_NUMBER", "value": "<exact verbatim substring>" } ] }
  ]
}

You are a certified legal translator working in a privacy-preserving document translation pipeline. You translate complete legal documents between ANY two languages - you will be told the exact source and target language codes for this job, never assume a fixed language pair.

Rules:
1. Produce a COMPLETE, formal legal-register translation. Never summarize, shorten, or omit content. Preserve the structure (numbered clauses, headings, line breaks) of the source.
2. The source text contains bracketed placeholder tokens such as [PERSON_1], [CASE_NUMBER_1], [ADDRESS_2]. These represent information that was deliberately removed before reaching you for privacy. Copy every token through to your translation completely unchanged, character-for-character, in the exact position it grammatically belongs in the translated sentence. Never translate, reorder the digits of, or alter a token.
3. If a glossary of required term translations is provided, you MUST use those exact target-language terms whenever the matching source term appears, for consistency across documents.
4. Track any new legal/institutional/document-specific term you had to translate without glossary guidance (e.g. a court name, a legal procedure name, a document title) and return it so it can be added to the glossary for future jobs. Do not include ordinary common words.
5. If you had to guess a translation because the source was ambiguous or partially illegible, say so explicitly rather than silently picking one option.

Output must strictly follow this JSON shape and contain nothing else:
{
  "translatedPages": [ { "pageNumber": <integer>, "translatedText": "<full translation of this page>" } ],
  "newGlossaryTerms": [ { "sourceTerm": "<term in source language>", "targetTerm": "<term in target language>", "context": "<short note on where/how it's used>" } ],
  "flaggedTranslations": [ "<short description of any guessed/ambiguous translation>" ]
}

={{ 'Source language: ' + $json.detectedLanguage + '
Target language: ' + $json.targetLanguage + '

Glossary (required term translations):
' + ($json.glossaryTerms.length ? $json.glossaryTerms.map(g => g.source + ' => ' + g.target).join('
') : '(none yet)') + '

Document text by page:

' + $json.tokenizedPages.map(p => '=== PAGE ' + p.pageNumber + ' ===
' + p.text).join('

') }}

parameters.formDescription: Review the original extracted text and the proposed translation below. Check that PII placeholders (e.g. [PERSON_1]) were correctly restored, the legal register is appropriate, and no content was dropped. Then enter your decision.

values.html:
={{ (() => { const ocrPages = $("Parse OCR Output").first().json.pages || []; const pipe = $('Detokenize PII').first().json; const finalPages = pipe.finalPages || []; const flagged = pipe.flaggedTranslations || []; let html = "
"; ocrPages.forEach((p, i) => { const tp = finalPages[i] || {}; html += "
Page " + p.pageNumber + "
Original (" + (pipe.detectedLanguage||"?") + ")Translation (" + (pipe.targetLanguage||"?") + ")
" + (p.text||"").substring(0,3000) + "" + (tp.text||"").substring(0,3000) + "

"; }); if (flagged.length) { html += "

⚠ Flagged by translator

    " + flagged.map(f => "
  • " + f + "
    • ").join("") + "
"; } html += "
"; return html; })() }}

values.fieldLabel: Corrected Translation (if Approve with Edits — paste full corrected text)
values.placeholder: Paste the complete corrected translation here. Leave blank if approving as-is.
Any notes on this translation, issues found, or reasons for rejection.

parameters.subject: ={{ "[Legal Translation] Translation rejected — needs rework: " + $json.fileName }}
parameters.message:
={{ "A translator has rejected the translation and flagged it for rework. File: " + $json.fileName + "
Reviewer: " + $json.reviewerName + "
Decision: " + $json.reviewDecision + "
Notes: " + ($json.reviewerNotes || "(none)") + " The file has been moved to the Needs-Review folder. Correct the translation and re-upload to re-process." }}

parameters.subject: =New Document Received — {{ $json.name || "Document" }}
parameters.message:
=

Document Received for Translation

A new document has been received and the AI translation pipeline is now processing it.

File Name{{ $json.name || "N/A" }}
Received At{{ $now.toLocaleString() }}
Status Processing — OCR, PII tokenization & AI translation in progress

You will receive another email when the translation is ready for your review.

Sent automatically by the Legal Translation Translation Pipeline

parameters.subject: =Duplicate File Skipped — {{ $json.FileName || "Document" }}

=

Duplicate Document Detected — Skipped

A file was uploaded that has already been processed. The pipeline skipped reprocessing to avoid duplicates.

File Name{{ $json.FileName || "N/A" }}
Hash Match{{ $json.FileHash || "N/A" }}
Originally Processed{{ $json.Timestamp || "N/A" }}
Certificate ID{{ $json.CertificateId || "N/A" }}
Existing Translation{{ $json.TranslationDocUrl ? 'View in Drive' : "N/A" }}

If you intended to reprocess this file, please delete the previous entry from the Processed Log and re-upload the file.

Sent automatically by the Legal Translation Translation Pipeline

parameters.subject: =Translation Ready for Review — {{ ($json.documentType||'document').replace(/_/g,' ').toUpperCase() }} ({{ ($json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }})
parameters.message:
=

Translation Ready for Human Review

The AI translation pipeline has completed processing and requires your review before the certified document is generated.

Document Type{{ ($json.documentType||'unknown').replace(/_/g,' ') }}
Language Pair{{ ($json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }}
File Name{{ $json.fileName || "N/A" }}
Pages{{ ($json.finalPages||[]).length }}
PII Tokens{{ $json.piiCount || 0 }} item(s) tokenized & restored
Flagged Items{{ ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).length > 0 ? ' ' + ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).length + ' term(s) require attention' : ' None' }}
Glossary Terms Added{{ ($json.newGlossaryTerms||[]).length }} new term(s) extracted
{{ ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).length > 0 ? '
Flagged Terms Needing Attention:
    ' + ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).map(f => '
  • ' + f + '
    • ').join('') + '
' : '' }}
Open Review Form

The pipeline is paused and waiting for your decision. The certified translation document will only be generated after approval.

If the button doesn't work, copy this URL: {{ $execution.resumeFormUrl }}

Sent automatically by the Legal Translation Translation Pipeline

parameters.subject: =Certified Translation Complete — {{ $json.certId || 'Document' }}
parameters.message:
=

Certified Translation Complete

The translation has been reviewed, approved, and the certified document has been saved to Google Drive.

Certificate ID{{ $json.certId || "N/A" }}
Document Type{{ ($json.documentType||'unknown').replace(/_/g,' ') }}
Language Pair{{ ($json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }}
Reviewed By{{ $json.reviewerName || "N/A" }}
Review Decision{{ $json.reviewDecision || "Approved" }}
Translation Date{{ $json.dateStr || $now.toISO() }}
Glossary Terms{{ ($json.newGlossaryTerms||[]).length }} new term(s) added to glossary
View Certified Translation in Drive
{{ $json.reviewerNotes && $json.reviewerNotes.trim() ? '
Reviewer Notes:
' + $json.reviewerNotes + '
' : '' }}

The original source file has been moved to the Archive folder. The Processed Log has been updated.

Sent automatically by the Legal Translation Translation Pipeline

Download PDF:
{{ $json.pdfDownloadUrl }}

parameters.content:
## AI-Assisted Legal Translation Pipeline
**7 stages, left to right:** Intake and dedup  →  OCR extraction  →  PII tokenization  →  Glossary-aware translation  →  Detokenization  →  Human review  →  Document assembly and delivery
The translation LLM never sees raw PII — names and addresses are replaced with placeholder tokens before any AI call and restored only after translation is complete.

**Stage 1 — Intake and Deduplication**
Drive trigger fires on new file in Inbox. SHA-256 hash computed for dedup — already-processed files are skipped with a notification. Parallel branch: moves file to Processing folder and emails intake confirmation.

**Stage 2 — OCR and Text Extraction**
GPT-4o vision extracts text verbatim per page, preserving reading order and flagging stamps, seals, and handwritten annotations. Returns structured JSON.

**Stage 3 — PII Tokenization  (Privacy Layer)**
GPT-4o detects names, addresses, phones, emails, and IDs. Each value is replaced with a stable token (e.g. PERSON_1). Token map lives only in execution memory — never logged. The translation LLM sees tokens only.

**Stage 4 — Glossary-Aware Translation and Detokenization**
Source language auto-detected. Established term pairs loaded from Glossary sheet, injected as required translations. GPT-4o translates in formal legal register — no summarization, tokens preserved verbatim, uncertain terms flagged. New terms appended to glossary. PII restored from token map; map then discarded.

**Stage 5 — Human Review**
Reviewer receives an email with a form link. Pipeline pauses (Wait node) until the form is submitted: Approve, Approve with Edits (reviewer pastes corrected text), or Reject. Approved proceeds to assembly; rejected goes to the Needs-Review branch.

**Stage 6 — Document Assembly and Delivery:** Certified translation built with cert ID, header, full translated content, and reviewer notes. Uploaded to Drive Approved folder. Logged to Processed Log. Source file archived. Completion email sent.
**Glossary Compounding:** New legal terms identified during translation are appended to the Glossary sheet. Translation consistency compounds across every document.
**Rejection Branch:** Rejection logged to Review Queue sheet. Team notified by email. Source file moved to Needs-Review folder for manual correction and re-upload to Inbox.
**Error Handling:** All fallible nodes route failures here. Error logged to Error Log sheet. Admin emailed with full details. Source file moved to Errors folder. Nothing fails silently.

parameters.subject: =Certified Translation Ready — {{ ($('Restore Pipeline').first().json.documentType||'document').replace(/_/g,' ') }} ({{ $('Restore Pipeline').first().json.certId || 'Case Document' }})
parameters.message:
=

Certified Translation Ready for Filing

This translation has been reviewed and approved by a qualified human reviewer.

Dear Counsel,

The certified translation below has been reviewed and approved. It is ready for submission to the relevant authority.

Certificate ID{{ $('Restore Pipeline').first().json.certId || "N/A" }}
Document Type{{ ($('Restore Pipeline').first().json.documentType || 'document').replace(/_/g,' ') }}
Language{{ ($('Restore Pipeline').first().json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($('Restore Pipeline').first().json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }}
Reviewer{{ $('Restore Pipeline').first().json.reviewerName || "N/A" }}
Review Decision{{ $('Restore Pipeline').first().json.reviewDecision || "Approved" }}
Translation Date{{ $('Restore Pipeline').first().json.dateStr || $now.format('MMMM D, YYYY') }}
View Certified Translation
Download PDF:
{{ $('Restore Pipeline').first().json.pdfDownloadUrl }}

Automated dispatch from the Legal Translation certified translation pipeline. Do not reply to this email.

values.content:
You are a certified legal translator working in a privacy-preserving document translation pipeline. You translate complete legal documents between ANY two languages - you will be told the exact source and target language codes for this job, never assume a fixed language pair.

Rules:
1. Produce a COMPLETE, formal legal-register translation. Never summarize, shorten, or omit content. Preserve the structure (numbered clauses, headings, line breaks) of the source.

Copy every token through to your translation completely unchanged, character-for-character, in the exact position it grammatically belongs in the translated sentence. Never translate, reorder the digits of, or alter a token.
3. If a glossary of required term translations is provided, you MUST use those exact target-language terms whenever the matching source term appears, for consistency across documents.
4. Track any new legal/institutional/document-specific term you had to translate without glossary guidance (e.g. a court name, a legal procedure name, a document title) and return it so it can be added to the glossary for future jobs. Do not include ordinary common words.
5. If you had to guess a translation because the source was ambiguous or partially illegible, say so explicitly rather than silently picking one option.
6. A human reviewer has reviewed the previous translation and provided specific correction notes. You MUST address every point raised in the REVIEWER CORRECTION NOTES below before all else.

Output must strictly follow this JSON shape and contain nothing else:
{
  "translatedPages": [ { "pageNumber": <integer>, "translatedText": "<full translation of this page>" } ],
  "newGlossaryTerms": [ { "sourceTerm": "<term in source language>", "targetTerm": "<term in target language>", "context": "<short note on where/how it's used>" } ],
  "flaggedTranslations": [ "<short description of any guessed/ambiguous translation>" ]
}

={{ 'Source language: ' + $json.detectedLanguage + '
Target language: ' + $json.targetLanguage + '

Glossary (required term translations):
' + ($json.glossaryTerms && $json.glossaryTerms.length ? $json.glossaryTerms.map(g => g.source + ' => ' + g.target).join('
') : '(none yet)') + '

REVIEWER CORRECTION NOTES (address every point in your retranslation):
' + ($json.reviewerNotes || 'No specific notes provided. Improve overall accuracy and legal register.') + '

Document text by page:

' + ($json.tokenizedPages || []).map(p => '=== PAGE ' + p.pageNumber + ' ===
' + p.text).join('

') }}

parameters.subject: =Retranslation Ready — Retry #{{ $json.retryCount }} — {{ ($json.documentType||'document').replace(/_/g,' ').toUpperCase() }} ({{ ($json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }})
parameters.message:
=

Retranslation Ready for Second Review

The document was rejected in the first review and has been retranslated with your correction notes applied. This is the final automated retry — if rejected again, it will be escalated to manual handling.

Retry #{{ $json.retryCount }} of 1 allowed
Document Type{{ ($json.documentType||'unknown').replace(/_/g,' ') }}
Language Pair{{ ($json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }}
Your Prior Notes{{ $json.reviewerNotes || '(none provided)' }}
Translator Flags{{ ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).length > 0 ? ' ' + ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).length + ' item(s) still flagged' : ' No new flags' }}
Final Retry: Rejecting again will escalate this document to manual translation.
Open Retry Review Form

The pipeline is paused awaiting your decision.

parameters.formDescription: This retranslation incorporates your previous correction notes. If you reject again, the document will be escalated to manual translation — no further automated retries.

values.html:
={{ (() => { const ocrPages = $("Parse OCR Output").first().json.pages || []; const finalPages = $("Detokenize Retry").first().json.finalPages || $json.finalPages || []; const flagged = pipe.flaggedTranslations || []; const priorNotes = $("Check Retry Count").first().json.reviewerNotes || ''; let html = "
"; if (priorNotes) { html += "
Your previous correction notes (applied to this retranslation):
" + priorNotes + "
"; } ocrPages.forEach((p, i) => { const tp = finalPages[i] || {}; html += "
Page " + p.pageNumber + "
Original (" + (pipe.detectedLanguage||"?") + ")Retranslation (" + (pipe.targetLanguage||"?") + ")
" + (p.text||"").substring(0,3000) + "" + (tp.text||"").substring(0,3000) + "

"; }); if (flagged.length) { html += "

⚠ Still flagged by translator

    " + flagged.map(f => "
  • " + f + "
    • ").join("") + "
"; } html += "
"; return html; })() }}

values.fieldLabel: Corrected Translation (if Approve with Edits — paste full corrected text)
values.placeholder: Paste the complete corrected translation here. Leave blank if approving as-is.
Notes on this retranslation. If rejecting, provide details — this will trigger manual escalation.

parameters.subject: =ESCALATION: Manual Translation Required — {{ ($json.documentType||'document').replace(/_/g,' ').toUpperCase() }}
parameters.message:
=

Document Escalated — Manual Translation Required

This document has exhausted all automated retry attempts and requires manual certified translation.

File{{ $json.fileName || 'Unknown' }}
Document Type{{ ($json.documentType||'unknown').replace(/_/g,' ') }}
Language Pair{{ ($json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }}
Retries Used{{ $json.retryCount || 1 }}
Reviewer Notes{{ $json.reviewerNotes || '(none)' }}
LocationNeeds-Review folder in Google Drive

Action Required: Retrieve the document from the Needs-Review Google Drive folder and arrange manual certified translation. Log completion in the Review Queue sheet.

    " + flagged.map(f => "
  • " + f + "
    • ").join("") + "
"; } html += "
"; return html; })() }}

parameters.content:
## AI-Assisted Legal Translation Pipeline
**7 stages, left to right:** Intake and dedup  →  OCR extraction  →  PII tokenization  →  Glossary-aware translation  →  Detokenization  →  Human review  →  Document assembly and delivery
The translation LLM never sees raw PII — names and addresses are replaced with placeholder tokens before any AI call and restored only after translation is complete.

**Stage 1 — Intake and Deduplication**
Drive trigger fires on new file in Inbox. SHA-256 hash computed for dedup — already-processed files are skipped with a notification. Parallel branch: moves file to Processing folder and emails intake confirmation.

**Stage 2 — OCR and Text Extraction**
GPT-4o vision extracts text verbatim per page, preserving reading order and flagging stamps, seals, and handwritten annotations. Returns structured JSON.

**Stage 3 — PII Tokenization  (Privacy Layer)**
GPT-4o detects names, addresses, phones, emails, and IDs. Each value is replaced with a stable token (e.g. PERSON_1). Token map lives only in execution memory — never logged. The translation LLM sees tokens only.

**Stage 4 — Glossary-Aware Translation and Detokenization**
Source language auto-detected. Established term pairs loaded from Glossary sheet, injected as required translations. GPT-4o translates in formal legal register — no summarization, tokens preserved verbatim, uncertain terms flagged. New terms appended to glossary. PII restored from token map; map then discarded.

**Stage 5 — Human Review**
Reviewer receives an email with a form link. Pipeline pauses (Wait node) until the form is submitted: Approve, Approve with Edits (reviewer pastes corrected text), or Reject. Approved proceeds to assembly; rejected goes to the Needs-Review branch.

**Stage 6 — Document Assembly and Delivery:** Certified translation built with cert ID, header, full translated content, and reviewer notes. Uploaded to Drive Approved folder. Logged to Processed Log. Source file archived. Completion email sent.
**Glossary Compounding:** New legal terms identified during translation are appended to the Glossary sheet. Translation consistency compounds across every document.
**Rejection Branch:** Rejection logged to Review Queue sheet. Team notified by email. Source file moved to Needs-Review folder for manual correction and re-upload to Inbox.
**Error Handling:** All fallible nodes route failures here. Error logged to Error Log sheet. Admin emailed with full details. Source file moved to Errors folder. Nothing fails silently.

values.content:
You are a certified legal translator working in a privacy-preserving document translation pipeline. You translate complete legal documents between ANY two languages - you will be told the exact source and target language codes for this job, never assume a fixed language pair.

Rules:
1. Produce a COMPLETE, formal legal-register translation. Never summarize, shorten, or omit content. Preserve the structure (numbered clauses, headings, line breaks) of the source.
2. The source text contains bracketed placeholder tokens such as [PERSON_1], [CASE_NUMBER_1], [ADDRESS_2]. These represent information that was deliberately removed before reaching you for privacy. Copy every token through to your translation completely unchanged, character-for-character, in the exact position it grammatically belongs in the translated sentence. Never translate, reorder the digits of, or alter a token.
3. If a glossary of required term translations is provided, you MUST use those exact target-language terms whenever the matching source term appears, for consistency across documents.
4. Track any new legal/institutional/document-specific term you had to translate without glossary guidance (e.g. a court name, a legal procedure name, a document title) and return it so it can be added to the glossary for future jobs. Do not include ordinary common words.
5. If you had to guess a translation because the source was ambiguous or partially illegible, say so explicitly rather than silently picking one option.
6. A human reviewer has reviewed the previous translation and provided specific correction notes. You MUST address every point raised in the REVIEWER CORRECTION NOTES below before all else.

Output must strictly follow this JSON shape and contain nothing else:
{
  "translatedPages": [ { "pageNumber": <integer>, "translatedText": "<full translation of this page>" } ],
  "newGlossaryTerms": [ { "sourceTerm": "<term in source language>", "targetTerm": "<term in target language>", "context": "<short note on where/how it's used>" } ],
  "flaggedTranslations": [ "<short description of any guessed/ambiguous translation>" ]
}

={{ 'Source language: ' + $json.detectedLanguage + '
Target language: ' + $json.targetLanguage + '

Glossary (required term translations):
' + ($json.glossaryTerms && $json.glossaryTerms.length ? $json.glossaryTerms.map(g => g.source + ' => ' + g.target).join('
') : '(none yet)') + '

REVIEWER CORRECTION NOTES (address every point in your retranslation):
' + ($json.reviewerNotes || 'No specific notes provided. Improve overall accuracy and legal register.') + '

Document text by page:

' + ($json.tokenizedPages || []).map(p => '=== PAGE ' + p.pageNumber + ' ===
' + p.text).join('

') }}

parameters.message:
=

Retranslation Ready for Second Review

The document was rejected in the first review and has been retranslated with your correction notes applied. This is the final automated retry — if rejected again, it will be escalated to manual handling.

Retry #{{ $json.retryCount }} of 1 allowed
Document Type{{ ($json.documentType||'unknown').replace(/_/g,' ') }}
Language Pair{{ ($json.detectedLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }} {{ ($json.targetLanguage||'?').toUpperCase() }}
Your Prior Notes{{ $json.reviewerNotes || '(none provided)' }}
Translator Flags{{ ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).length > 0 ? ' ' + ($json.flaggedTranslations||[]).length + ' item(s) still flagged' : ' No new flags' }}
Final Retry: Rejecting again will escalate this document to manual translation.
Open Retry Review Form

The pipeline is paused awaiting your decision.

parameters.formDescription: This retranslation incorporates your previous correction notes. If you reject again, the document will be escalated to manual translation — no further automated retries.

values.html:
={{ (() => { const ocrPages = $("Parse OCR Output").first().json.pages || []; const finalPages = $("Detokenize Retry").first().json.finalPages || $json.finalPages || []; const flagged = pipe.flaggedTranslations || []; const priorNotes = $("Check Retry Count").first().json.reviewerNotes || ''; let html = "
"; if (priorNotes) { html += "
Your previous correction notes (applied to this retranslation):
" + priorNotes + "
"; } ocrPages.forEach((p, i) => { const tp = finalPages[i] || {}; html += "
Page " + p.pageNumber + "
Original (" + (pipe.detectedLanguage||"?") + ")Retranslation (" + (pipe.targetLanguage||"?") + ")
" + (p.text||"").substring(0,3000) + "" + (tp.text||"").substring(0,3000) + "

"; }); if (flagged.length) { html += "

⚠ Still flagged by translator

    " + flagged.map(f => "
  • " + f + "
    • ").join("") + "
"; } html += "
"; return html; })() }}
